name: NABH Readiness Checklist
shortBenefit: Self-assess your hospital's readiness for NABH accreditation across all 10 chapters.
intro: Assess how prepared your hospital is for NABH accreditation (5th edition). Rate each requirement across the 10 NABH chapters and get an instant readiness score, chapter-wise compliance and a list of critical gaps to close before assessment.

seo.title: NABH Readiness Checklist (2026) | Free Hospital Accreditation Self-Assessment | Varada Nexus
seo.description: Free NABH readiness checklist for Indian hospitals. Self-assess accreditation preparedness across all 10 NABH chapters, get a compliance score and critical-gap report instantly.

items.text: Documented registration, admission and transfer/discharge process is in place
Initial assessment of every patient is documented within the defined timeframe
Defined process for patient referral and safe transfer to another facility
Uniform care is guided by written policies and clinical protocols
Emergency, resuscitation (code blue) and ambulance services are defined and drilled
Documented policies for high-risk procedures, surgery and anaesthesia
Formulary and documented policy for procurement, storage and prescribing of medication
High-alert and look-alike/sound-alike (LASA) drugs are identified and controlled
Adverse drug event / medication error reporting system exists
Patient rights and responsibilities are documented and displayed
Informed consent process is defined for procedures requiring it
Process to obtain patient feedback and handle grievances
Infection control programme, committee and manual are in place
Hand hygiene and biomedical waste management as per BMW Rules 2016
Surveillance of hospital-associated infections (HAI) with monthly rates
Structured quality improvement programme with defined indicators
Incident reporting, sentinel-event and root-cause-analysis (RCA) system
International Patient Safety Goals (IPSG) implemented
Defined organogram, governance and roles/responsibilities
Valid statutory licences and registrations maintained
Fire and emergency preparedness plan with mock drills
Preventive maintenance programme for equipment and utilities
Medical gas, electrical and water safety systems verified
Documented recruitment, credentialing and privileging process
Ongoing training, BLS/ACLS and induction records maintained
Health checks and personnel files for all staff
Complete, accurate and standardised medical records with retention policy
Confidentiality, integrity and security of patient data ensured

assumptions: This is a simplified self-assessment covering representative requirements from the 10 NABH 5th-edition hospital chapters — not the full standard's objective elements. Each item scores Yes=1, Partial=0.5, No=0; the overall score is the average across all items. 'Critical gaps' counts critical items not fully compliant. Use it to prioritise, not to certify.
disclaimer: This checklist is an informal readiness indicator only and does NOT constitute NABH accreditation, a gap audit, or professional quality/regulatory advice. It is not affiliated with or endorsed by NABH/QCI. Formal accreditation requires assessment against the complete official NABH standard by NABH. Varada Nexus accepts no liability for decisions made using this tool.

faq.a: NABH (National Accreditation Board for Hospitals & Healthcare Providers), a constituent board of QCI, accredits hospitals in India against defined quality and patient-safety standards. The current hospital standard is the 5th edition.

faq.q: How many chapters are there in NABH?
faq.a: The NABH 5th edition hospital standard is organised into 10 chapters covering patient-centred and organisation-centred standards, from Access & Assessment to Information Management.

faq.q: What score means we are ready for a NABH assessment?
faq.a: As a rough guide, 85%+ with no open critical gaps suggests you are close to assessment-ready; 70–84% means near ready; below 70% indicates significant preparation is still needed. A formal gap audit is recommended before applying.

faq.q: Does this replace a formal NABH gap audit?
faq.a: No. This is a quick self-assessment for prioritisation. A formal, standard-by-standard gap audit by qualified consultants is needed before applying for accreditation.
